Quick Facts
  • Parents are David Cook and Susan Cook-Loustalot
  • Has a sister Karissa and a younger brother, Kobi
Career Highlights
  • Pan American Games, Toronto, 2015 (Team - Men)
  • Junior World Championships, 2011 (Team - Men)

Quick Facts
  • Daughter of Bill and Jill Cook

  • Has two siblings, Lee and Kelsey

  • Works full-time as a contracting officer for the federal government, buying equipment to fulfill the nation’s military missions.
  • Diagnosed with aniridia, a rare genetic condition in which the iris does not develop in the eye
  • Got involved in goalball in 2016 when a co-worker invited her to a goalball practice at the Ohio State School for the Blind

  • Attended her first goalball tournament in Michigan as a pool player in 2017, where she fell in love with the sport and the goalball community and has been playing ever since

  • Hobbies include goalball, running, adventuring new places in Columbus, traveling, cooking, and being with friends and family

  • Is left-handed and enjoys a good cup of coffee.

    • Paralympic Games Tokyo 2020, silver (Team - Women)
World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2022 – 6th (Team - Women)
  • Years of participation: Team - Women 2022
  • Top finish: 6th – 2022 (Team - Women)

USABA

Quick Facts
  • Daughter of Kevin and Laura Cook. Has five siblings: Kevin, Kara, Kelsey, Kylie and Kendall
  • Started diving at the age of three, and her sister, Kara, was a diver at Purdue University
  • Says she is extremely good at Games of Thrones and Harry Potter trivia
  • Narrowly missed making the 2012 Olympic team by .42 points and then was away from competition for two years while recovering from shoulder and knee surgeries
  • Says the hardest part about training is the mental aspect
  • During competition, she likes to dance and listen to music. Migos, Cardi B, Travis Scott and Taylor Swift are among her favorites) between dives
  • Favorite dive is 305B
  • Hobbies include cooking, reading, hiking, watching Netflix and playing other sports
  • Current synchro partner is Sarah Bacon

Home Town: Kenai, Alaska
Current Location: Jericho, Vermont  

Did you know….?

My dream vacation spot is…. somewhere warm, with grass huts on the beach 
When traveling, the thing I miss the most is….. good ol' 'merican food 
My favorite meal is…. home-cooked one 
I never leave the house without….. way too many layers 
If I were not an athlete, I would…..have to get a real job

MORE ON TRAVIS: Travis started competitively skiing in the seventh grade for his local middle school. He then skied 4 years varsity at Kenai Central High School along with three trips to the Junior National Cross Country Ski Championships representing Team Alaska. His biathlon career started when he joined the National Guard his senior year of high school. He has been training and racing on their team since October of 2015, making two junior world teams. He accredits the National Guard for his success in the last few years and continues to give them praise for the amount of support and commitment they have shown to him as an athlete.

USA Swimming

#22
Height: 5'10"
Position: 1B - R/R
Hometown: Diamond Bar, California
School: UCLA
Graduation: 1984


A FEW ACCOMPLISHMENTS

Two-time Olympic Gold Medalist (1996, 2000)
Three-time World Champion (1990, 1994, 1998)
Four-time Pan American Gold Medalist (1987, 1991, 1995, 1999) Member of the National Softball Hall of Fame and ISF Hall of Fame
Earned ASA All-American honors 16 times

EXPERIENCE

2000: Gold medalist at Olympic Games in Sydney, Australia
1999: Gold medalist at Pan American Games, U.S. Olympic Cup and Canada Cup
- Hit .355 with 11 hits, six RBI and four runs scored (Pan Am)
1998: Gold medalist at ISF World Championships
- Hit .345 with four home runs and 12 RBI
1997: Silver medalist at Superball in Columbus, Ga.
1996: Gold medalist at Olympic Games in Atlanta, Georgia
- Hit .393 with three home runs and nine RBI
1995: Gold medalist at Pan American Games and Superball in Columbus, Ga.
- Led hitters with .581 average and eight RBI (Pan Am)
1994: Gold medalist at ISF World Championships and Pan American Games Qualifier
- Hit .357 with two home runs and five RBI
1993: Gold medalist at Intercontinental Cup in Holland
1992: Gold medalist at Women's World Challenger Cup
1991: Gold medalist at Pan American Games
1990: Gold medalist at ISF World Championship
- Hit .536 with eight RBI
1987: Gold medalist at Pan American Games
- Hit .389 with six RBI
1983: Silver medalist at Pan American Games
- Hit .464 with nine RBI

AT UCLA

Two-time NCAA National Champion (1982, 1984)
Named to Pac-10 All-Decade team

GETTING TO KNOW DOUTY

Named an ASA All-American 16 times during Women's Major Fast pitch career
Seven-time ASA National Champion
Born on February 26, 1962

Career Highlights
  • Silver medalist (10-meter) at 2018 FINA Grand Prix/Bolzano
